headline: Dollar ripping, rates sticky, Fed done cutting—risk assets face a slow bleed unless a shock forces Fed's hand.
invest_in: US Treasuries (especially 10Y) — rates are peaking, first cuts eventually coming; lock in 4.5%+ now
Gold & gold miners — strong dollar doesn't last forever, and inflation breakevens (2.35%) are firm; gold hedges the debasement cycle you know is coming when Fed pivots
Cash in short-duration instruments (T-bills, money market) — 3.6%+ risk-free with fragile plumbing = optionality to deploy on the next shock
Put options on high-beta (tech, semis, crypto) — VIX is presumably higher than 15 now, but wait for volatility crush if we chop; buy cheap 3-month puts on SPY/QQQ for black-swan insurance
JNK on dips only if spreads widen — you noted JNK at 9% yield is attractive, but exit immediately if liquidity cracks (watch OAS for signs of widening spike)
Quality dividend compounders / 'boring stocks that make the backbone' — healthcare, utilities, staples with pricing power; they hold up better in slow bleeds
BTC on capitulation dips — post-halving (already happened), debasement thesis intact; only accumulate if funding plumbing data shows Fed about to pivot (don't chase now)
